data.title: Lundin Gold reported strong financial performance in 2025, with $1.78B revenues from 503,330 oz gold sales at $3,594/oz. Cash flow hit $926M, while gold production reached 498,315 oz. Investment in exploration and expansion of high-grade deposits noted, alongside $664M dividends and $1.15 dividend per share this year. Lundin Gold exits 2025 debt-free.
data.description: Lundin Gold Inc. reported significant financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. The company achieved record-breaking gold production of 498,315 oz with $926 million in free cash flow. Revenues reached $1.78 billion from sales of 503,330 oz at an average realized gold price of $3,594 per oz. The Fruta del Norte mine in Ecuador showed strong performance, with cash operating costs of $838/oz and all-in sustaining costs of $1,015/oz. Lundin Gold also returned a record $664 million in dividends during the year. The company conducted a successful exploration program, drilling 121,519 meters, showcasing potential for growth within the land package. With no debt and a growing pipeline of opportunities, Lundin Gold emphasizes disciplined execution and responsible growth. Key operational figures for 2025 included 498,315 oz of gold produced, 5,009 tpd average throughput, and 89.0% average recovery rate. Lundin Gold's future looks promising with a focus on strategic flexibility and continued growth.

data.title: Annual "Destination Professionals Day" on Feb 19, 2026, celebrated worldwide by Destinations International, representing CVBs.
data.description: International attention is being drawn to a special observance as events and initiatives are taking place in Potomac Falls, Virginia. Destinations International (DI), a prominent association for destination organizations and convention and visitors bureaus (CVBs), is recognizing this observance today, February 19, 2026. This acknowledgment is part of a series of events happening globally to highlight the significance of the observance.

data.title: Philanthropic partnership funds research in low-income countries to evaluate AI tools in health. 3 foundations involved.
data.description: Three prominent foundations – the Gates Foundation, Novo Nordisk Foundation, and Wellcome – are teaming up to fund evaluations of AI tools in low- and middle-income countries. The inaugural call for proposals is currently accepting submissions and is specifically looking for mature AI tools that could enhance health outcomes. This collaborative initiative aims to support locally driven efforts and foster advancements in the healthcare sector.

The decrease in growth is attributed to trade tensions between major economies and a slowdown in manufacturing. In addition, global trade is expected to expand by only 2.6% this year, down from the initial estimate of 3%. The report also highlights the impact of geopolitical uncertainties and policy challenges on economic growth around the world.

data.title: Blue Hat Interactive priced US$6.4M public offering of 32M Units at US$0.20 each, expecting gross proceeds of US$6.4M.
data.description: In Xiamen, China, on February 19, 2026, Blue Hat Interactive Entertainment Technology (NASDAQ: BHAT) revealed details about its underwritten public offering. The Offering consists of 32,000,000 Units priced at US$0.20 per Unit, leading to an expected gross income of about US$6.4 million for the Company, before accounting for underwriting deductions and other costs. Each Unit comprises one Ordinary Share valued at US$0.0000001 per share and one Warrant, with the latter being initially exercisable for one Ordinary Share.

data.title: Foundation for Drug-Free World distributes 1M booklets in Italy during Winter Olympics to combat drug abuse. #MilanoCortina2026
data.description: In Milan on February 19, 2026, the Foundation for a Drug-Free World achieved a significant milestone by distributing over 1,000,000 The Truth About Drugs booklets throughout Italy during the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics.
